notes: Codex now records all three variants via drive-codex-interactive.sh (sigkill + restart primitives), so the SIGKILL arcs are observable end-to-end. Phase order follows the recipe's variant chain — variant 1 (clean Ctrl-D exit) → variant 2 (SIGKILL mid-idle) → variant 3 (SIGKILL mid-turn). Each variant is born as a proc-<PID> pre-session in ready (vN_session_birth), the rollout session reconciles under a new session_id (vN_handoff, asserted never to enter waiting over its lifetime), then the rollout drives ready→working (vN_turn_start). Each variant's process_exited anchors the next variant's birth. Spec assertions: v1 'State is ready at exit'; v2 'state never re-enters working/waiting once idle'; v3 'process exits while still working (does not stick)'. Variant 3's recipe sleeps 5 s before the kill (vs claudecode's 2 s) because codex's ready→working promotion lags ~2.7 s behind first activity — a shorter window would kill before working is observed and the variant would collapse to ready→process_exited.

text: Variant 1: pre-session appears in ready within 1s of recording start
Variant 1: rollout session reconciles (different session_id); never enters waiting through end of its lifetime
Variant 1: first user prompt drives the rollout session ready → working
Variant 1: turn completes back to ready
Variant 1: clean Ctrl-D exit — process_exited fires on the rollout session AFTER state returned to ready (the v1_turn_done → v1_exit chain proves 'State is ready at exit')
Variant 2: new pre-session appears in ready after variant 1 finishes
Variant 2: rollout session reconciles (different session_id); never enters waiting over its lifetime
Variant 2: user prompt drives the rollout ready → working

Variant 2: turn completes back to ready; over the ensuing idle window (through exit) the rollout never re-enters working or waiting (satisfies 'state never entered working post-turn before the SIGKILL')
Variant 2: SIGKILL mid-idle — process_exited fires on the rollout while it is in ready
Variant 3: new pre-session appears in ready after variant 2 finishes
Variant 3: rollout session reconciles (different session_id); never enters waiting over its lifetime
Variant 3: user prompt drives the rollout ready → working
Variant 3: SIGKILL mid-turn — process_exited fires on the rollout while it is still in working (live sweep-to-ready may not appear in the recording's 8 s tail per the recipe's notes)
